resources.string: PlayTranslate uses the Accessibility Service to take screenshots of the game screen and show a floating overlay icon. No UI content, input events, or personal data are read or monitored.
For translating game text on screen

PlayTranslate needs the Accessibility permission to capture screenshots of the game screen on the top display while staying open on the bottom display.

PlayTranslate does not read any UI content, monitor input, or access any personal data — it only takes screenshots when you tap Translate or start live mode.

Go to Settings → Accessibility → Installed apps → PlayTranslate → Enable.
Enable PlayTranslate in Accessibility Settings first
Tap Translate to capture the game screen
Long-press Regions or Auto for quick-select menus
Your companion app for playing games in other languages.

Playing something you don\'t understand? Turn on-screen text into translations instantly.\n\nLearning a language? Word-by-word definitions, reading guides (furigana, pinyin), and one-tap Anki flashcards.
Display-over-other-apps permission is required for Share Screen mode.
Screen sharing was not granted. Try again from Settings.

Android requires apps that run in the background to display a status bar notification while active.\n\nThis will never send alerts, sounds, or banners — only a quiet persistent indicator that PlayTranslate is running.
Look for PlayTranslate under Downloaded apps.

PlayTranslate uses Accessibility to:
• Capture the game screen
• Show translated text over your game
• Look up words by dragging over game text
• Display game screen controls

It does not read UI content, monitor input, or access personal data.

From PlayTranslate\'s Android settings page, select the button on the top right with three dots (⋮) and then select \"Allow restricted settings\". After authenticating, you should be able to turn on accessibility permissions.
To see a preview of your selected region on the game screen, you must enable Accessibility Settings. This is also necessary to create new capture regions. Tap here to change.

Adding custom regions requires the Accessibility permission. It\'s used to draw a live preview box on the game screen so you can see exactly what area you\'re selecting.\n\nGo to Settings → Accessibility → Installed apps → PlayTranslate → Enable.
Couldn\'t load more examples — check your connection.
Sentences from Tatoeba · CC BY 2.0 FR
Select the display that shows the game. Usually Display 0 is the main/top screen.
Paste your DeepL API key here
⚠ No internet connection — showing offline translation (lower quality).
⚠ Translation service unavailable — showing offline translation (lower quality).
Drag the region box on the game screen to select your desired area. Drag the top or bottom edge, or drag the middle to move the whole box.
• Drag — Look up words by dragging over game text\n• Hold — Preview translations\n• Tap — Open quick menu
• Drag — Look up words by dragging over game text\n• Hold — Preview translations\n• Tap — Open quick menu
The floating overlay icon requires the Accessibility permission to draw on the game screen.\n\nGo to Settings → Accessibility → Installed apps → PlayTranslate → Enable.

PlayTranslate needs the Accessibility permission to work on single-screen devices.

With it, PlayTranslate can:
• Capture the game screen without leaving your app
• Show translated text as an overlay over your game
• Look up words by dragging over game text
• Display game screen controls

It does not read UI content, monitor input, or access personal data.
Save words you discover while playing and review them later.\n\nPlayTranslate can save translated sentences, vocabulary, and screenshots directly to AnkiDroid, a free spaced-repetition flashcard app.
Are you a language learner? Create flashcards in %1$s from unknown words and sentences, and review them later using AnkiDroid, a free and popular spaced-repetition flashcard app.
To add flashcards to Anki, PlayTranslate needs permission to access AnkiDroid.\n\nTap Continue to grant this permission.
Permission denied — grant it in AnkiDroid settings
Failed to add card to Anki
